p3.title: Generated pack risk class derived solely from the first unit
p3.detail: buildGeneratedPacks() classifies the entire pack via classifyUnitRisk(units[start]) (first unit only). CP00-095 leads with 'Scope inventory' (Risk C), so the 150-unit pack is Risk C even though it contains permission/audit/tenant precheck units that individually key to Risk A. For CP00-095 this is acceptable and does NOT weaken gates: every included unit is non-enforcing foundational scaffolding (permission precheck is reference_only/evaluated:false, audit precheck is hint_only/writes_audit_event:false), no-write boundaries are enforced by contract + code + tests, and the Claude C01 / Hermes H01 gates apply to the full pack.
p3.recommendation: In a future generator revision, classify a generated pack by the maximum risk across all its units so a pack that leads with a scaffolding unit but contains genuinely enforcing security units cannot be under-classified. Non-blocking; this is the established generator rule (law-firm-os.closeout-pack-plan.generator.v1) and not introduced by this pack.

p3.title: service intake synthetic_only flag is informational, not fail-closed
p3.detail: normalizeCoreDomainServiceRequest sets synthetic_only: input.synthetic_only !== false, so a caller may pass synthetic_only:false without rejection. The synthetic_data_only invariant and accepts_real_client_data:false still hold structurally because no code path persists or processes real data (writes_product_state:false, no DB/IO in the domain package), so the boundary is not actually breachable here.
p3.recommendation: To make synthetic_data_only an actively enforced invariant rather than an advisory marker, reject requests where synthetic_only === false. Non-blocking.

risk_c_150_unit_acceleration_preserves_q.detail: 150 units sits at the upper bound of the Risk C range and is flagged within_default_risk_range:true. Acceleration does not weaken gates: Claude C01 and Hermes H01 still apply to the full pack, domain tests (13) and the full suite (931) pass, rp01:core-domain:validate and closeout-pack-plan:validate exit 0, and all content is non-enforcing foundational scaffolding. See finding CP00-095-P3-01 for the advisory on first-unit-only risk classification.

no_runtime_permission_evaluation.detail: createCoreDomainPermissionPrecheck returns status:'reference_only', evaluated:false and never branches on permissionRef.effect to allow/deny. Contract no_write_boundaries.evaluates_runtime_permission:false and service_entrypoint_contract.evaluates_runtime_permission:false. Permission evaluation explicitly deferred to RP02. Tests assert permission_precheck.evaluated === false.

no_audit_ledger_writes.detail: createCoreDomainAuditHint returns status:'hint_only', writes_audit_event:false. createAuditEvent/createAuditEventReference are in-memory frozen-record constructors only (no fs/network/DB). Contract no_write_boundaries.writes_audit_event:false and service_entrypoint_contract.writes_audit_event:false. Audit ledger write explicitly deferred to RP03. Tests assert audit_hint.writes_audit_event === false.

hrx_remains_embedded.detail: generate-closeout-pack-plan.mjs expanded_product_scope keeps hrx_embedded_people_units:901 with hrx_boundary:'embedded_people_hr_evidence_module_inside_law_firm_os'. CP00-095 covers only RP01 core-domain units and does not split, extract, or relocate HRX.

ldip_not_split_or_implemented.detail: No reviewed CP00-095 file implements or splits LDIP; the pack scope is RP01.P00.M00.S01-RP01.P02.M04.S06 (core domain foundation). Note: an untracked working-tree artifact docs/ldip-integration/claude-review-results/c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt-01.json exists, but it is outside this pack's deliverable set and is an 'invalid-attempt' review result, not an LDIP implementation. Out of scope for CP00-095; not a finding.

no_real_sensitive_data.detail: All identifiers in tests and validator are synthetic (t_rp01, u_owner, m_rp01, c_rp01, d_rp01, dv_rp01_v1, perm_rp01_read, *_validator). No real client/matter/document/billing data, no credentials, secrets, API keys, tokens, or real emails. Contract no_write_boundaries.uses_real_client_data:false; invariant synthetic_data_only present; service_entrypoint_contract.accepts_real_client_data:false. (See CP00-095-P3-02 re: synthetic_only flag enforcement.)

no_write_boundaries_enforced.detail: Domain package is pure functions with no persistence/IO. assembleCoreDomainServiceIntake and the contract both expose writes_product_state:false; contract no_write_boundaries sets creates_database_rows:false, writes_product_state:false. Only file IO in the change set is a read in validate-rp01-core-domain-contract.mjs.

pack_identity_consistency.detail: pack_id CP00-095, risk_class C, unit_count 150, range RP01.P00.M00.S01-RP01.P02.M04.S06 are consistent across CORE_DOMAIN_PACK_BINDING, CORE_DOMAIN_SERVICE_ENTRYPOINT_CONTRACT, contracts/core-domain-contract.json, next-pack-queue.json, and the plan snapshot. Queue correctly starts after live CP00-094 at live_next_unit_id RP01.P00.M00.S01 as the first generated pack.

contract_code_consistency.detail: contract.owns (24), entity_definitions (24), owner_boundaries (4+2+8+6+2+2=24), and ownership.js CORE_DOMAIN_ENTITY_DEFINITIONS (24) agree. matter_traceable_entity_types (8) + pre_matter_entity_types (16) = 24. validate-rp01-core-domain-contract.mjs cross-checks binding, owners, traceability, registry, and metadata-only intake and exits 0.

tests_and_validators_green.detail: node --check on all new/changed JS exit 0; domain tests 13 passed; npm test 931 passed; rp01:core-domain:validate exit 0 (entities 24, entrypoint core_domain_service_intake); npm run validate, closeout-pack-plan:validate (796 packs, 53547 units, queue after CP00-094), spec/weighted/fullplan/goal-closeout validators, build, and git diff --check all exit 0.

deferrals_explicit.detail: contract.deferred_to_future_rps explicitly records permission_evaluation->RP02, audit_ledger_write->RP03, matter_workflow_runtime->RP05, dms_document_runtime->RP06, and invariants permission_reference_only_until_rp02 / audit_hint_only_until_rp03 are declared. Out-of-pack runtime behavior is explicitly deferred, not silently omitted.

working_tree_hygiene.detail: Untracked working-tree noise (.DS_Store, docs/.DS_Store, 'Law Firm OS UI/', docs/ldip-integration/...invalid-attempt-01.json, docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md) is present but is not part of CP00-095's deliverable file set and does not affect pack correctness. Ensure these are not committed into the pack; not a finding against CP00-095.
